message.content:
你是架構探索 agent。需求：「設計題目：讓 telegram-kiro-bridge 在切換不同 ACP backend（Kiro CLI / Codex / Claude Agent）時，修改程式碼後都能遵守統一流程並產出「量化自評分數」，取代目前只有質性判定（合理性審查/對抗驗證 needs-attention）的現狀。

## 背景與既有架構限制（設計必須尊重）

1. Preamble 每個 session 只 build 一次並凍結（POLICIES/preamble-frozen-snapshot.md），三個 backend 收到同一份文字，中途不可重建。
2. Claude Code 專屬機制（Skill 工具呼叫、Workflow 多 agent 編排、PreToolUse Hook 強制擋修改）對 Kiro CLI / Codex backend 無效——PreToolUse Hook 跨 CLI 投影已明確否決過（decision-gate-hook-minimal）。
3. 目前唯一真正跨三個 backend 生效的機制是「純文字層指令 + 裸 token 協定」：模型讀 preamble 文字自律照做，回覆中裸寫 token（如既有 `<<SKILL_USED:name|reason>>`），bridge 端 `src/observerTransformer.ts` 用正則抓取記錄，不依賴任何 CLI 專屬工具。
4. 現有品質機制都是質性判定，非量化：Section 9 合理性審查（5 點自問）、Section 11 事實主張閘門（證據分級）、R-5（連續 2 輪 needs-attention 熔斷降級）。

## 初步構想（已用 Fable 5 brainstorm 過一輪，作為候選方案之一，非定案）

- 六維度勾選制計分（型別驗證/功能驗證/呼叫端影響/範圍紀律/完成度/回讀驗證，各占 25/20/20/15/10/10 分），證據先行、總分只能從勾選算出（防止模型跳過檢查直接寫高分）。
- 閾值 ≥80 通過，60-79 只重做未過項（上限2輪）、<60 或重做仍未過併入既有熔斷機制（不新增第二套計數器）。
- 新 token `<<SELF_EVAL:total|breakdown|evidence>>` 跨三 backend 落地。
- 誠實邊界：這終究是自評，沒有 hook 能強制非 Claude backend 誠實作答；唯一 model-independent 的驗證點是 bridge 端收到 token 後自跑 `npx tsc --noEmit` 覆核型別驗證維度，宣稱通過但實跑失敗即標記 discrepancy。
- 建議誠實分級而非假裝三個 backend 同等保證：L1（Kiro CLI/Codex）=文字自評+bridge tsc 覆核；L2（Claude Agent）=L1 之上疊加 `/dev-review` 異源 skeptic 重算分數，分差>15 以低分為準。

## 要 dev-design workflow 產出

1. 至少 3 個互相競爭的設計方案（不必受限於上述初步構想，可推翻重來），涵蓋：評分維度與計分邏輯放在哪裡（preamble 純文字 vs 部分交給 bridge 端程式碼覆核）、token 格式與 `src/observerTransformer.ts` 的整合方式（比照 `SKILL_USED_RE` 的正則+長度上限模式）、閾值與重做/熔斷整合方式、跨 backend 保證等級是否要誠實分級。
2. 對抗驗證找出致命缺陷（例如：模型灌水空間、token 被使用者手動觸發誤判、與既有熔斷計數器衝突、preamble 字數膨脹代價、bridge 端覆核 tsc 的效能/併發代價、Kiro CLI 是否真的會遵守純文字指令等）。
3. 最終設計規格（含 BC-x 可驗證行為契約），明確標出要改動的檔案（preamble builder、observerTransformer.ts、sessionManager.ts 或等效覆核邏輯、CLAUDE.md 方法論新增章節）與各檔案改動範圍。

這是方法論修改（CLAUDE.md 新增章節）+ 跨 3+ 檔案的大型任務，需要完整 workflow 產出設計規格供後續實作與 R-2 cross-source review。」
探索專案現況（Read/Grep/Glob）：相關既有模組、架構模式、約束、需求未指定的 unknowns。

【承重核要求 — 必須遵守】
1. 事實求證（認知驗證）：任何「X 是 Y / 系統現在如何」的事實斷言，先分級證據：
   🟢 A 級（檔:行原文）/ 🟡 B 級（間接）/ 🔴 反例檢查（若真應見X·若假應見Y·實際Z）。
   只有 A 級或 B 級+反例通過才可當設計前提；弱證據必須標「待驗證」，不可當既成事實設計。
2. 因果鏈（依賴影響）：設計觸及既有程式碼時，標出受影響的連動點（grep 呼叫者窮舉），
   呼叫者=0 的修改點要警示（可能有 inline 繞過）。
3. 誠實邊界：不確定就說不確定，不要為了完整性編造需求未指定的設計選擇。

回報：既有架構摘要 / 約束 / unknowns / 你做的事實斷言及證據級。

- claude-mem-curate: 把 claude-mem 抽出的 shortlist 精選成少量繁體中文、去重後寫進 AIMemory。當使用者說「精選/整理 claude-mem」「把最近重點存記憶」,或每日自動 ingestion 時使用。
- dual-skill-review-loop: Use when producing any deliverable that needs quality assurance — writing, reports, proposals, code designs — and you want the output to reach ≥95 score through structured self-review iterations
- excel-to-ai-doc: Convert image-heavy Excel game spec spreadsheets (.xlsx) into an AI-readable knowledge structure — Markdown text + extracted original images (anchored to their source cells) + metadata.json. Use when the user wants to turn a 規格書/spec/design xlsx into something an AI or agent can read, asks to "convert this spec to markdown", "make this xlsx AI-readable", "extract images and tables from this spreadsheet for analysis", or needs to feed a slot/game spec workbook to a vision model. Triggers on game design specs, paytables, UI/flow screenshots embedded in .xlsx. NOT for clean tabular data exports (use a plain csv/markdown converter) or non-xlsx files.
- huashu-slides: AI演示文稿全流程制作：内容结构化→设计选型→AI插画/HTML构建→PPTX导出。17种实战验证的视觉风格（漫画/极简/数据叙事/国风），可编辑HTML与全AI视觉两条路径自由选择。当用户提到"做PPT"、"制作幻灯片"、"演示文稿"、"Keynote"、"slides"、"课件"、"汇报材料"、"路演"时使用。
- knowhow-accumulation: Use when capturing lessons from AI failures into structured ❌→✅ records, when reviewing past interactions to extract reusable patterns, or when building a self-improving knowledge base from repeated mistakes
- memory-to-skill: 當使用者要求「檢視 session 資料夾並把內容整理成 skill」、需要從過去對話中抽取可重用模式、或要把剛剛完成的工作經驗固化成 skill 時使用
- ms-agent-scheduled-prompts: 當需要為 LLM agent bridge（Telegram bot、Slack bot、CLI UI）做延遲 / 常態性 prompt 觸發、排程 bridge 指令（如每天凌晨 /restart）、或要在 agent 回覆裡嵌 token 讓使用者自助建排程時使用
- ms-agent-text-token-signaling: 當 LLM agent 需要觸發 host 端動作（傳檔、開網址、執行指令）但協定不允許 agent 端發起自訂 RPC、或想在 streaming 文字回覆中加上 side effect 又不破壞聊天 UX 時使用
- ms-external-repo-absorption: ms-external-repo-absorption
- ms-kiro-strreplace-silent-fail: 當用 Kiro 的 strReplace 或 fs-edit 類工具改檔、tool 回報 Successfully replaced N occurrences 但事後 grep / read 發現磁碟內容沒變、或同一個檔案連續發幾個 strReplace 只有部分生效時使用
- ms-portable-skill-authoring: 當寫 skill 會被多個 agent CLI（Kiro / Codex / Claude）或多台機器共用、要避免把 F:\AI\AIMemory 或 ~/.kiro 這類絕對路徑寫死在 SKILL.md、或要讓 bridge 把環境差異注入成變數讓同一份 SKILL.md 跨機器重用時使用
- ms-streaming-token-pipeline: Use when a Telegram/Slack bot streams text via mid-turn edits plus a final render. Both paths must share one transform, or ASK buttons drop, half-tokens leak, and rate-limit recovery overwrites it.
- ms-telegram-ask-button-protocol: Use when emitting <<ASK:...>> for Telegram bridge buttons, or when an ASK token renders verbatim. Label soft cap 20 chars; >20 SHALL use numbered body list with short labels. See SKILL.md for limits.
- ms-telegram-bot-rate-limit-survival: 當 Telegram bot 對使用者 chat 串 streaming edit 或連發多則訊息、遇到 429 Too Many Requests / retry_after 愈累積愈長（60s→183s）、grammY autoRetry 超過 maxDelaySeconds 拋錯、或需要設計「限流期間凍結 UI + 限流結束後自動補發」時使用
- ms-wiki-knowledge-base: 當 agent bridge 要維運雙層記憶（facts + wiki 知識庫）時使用：執行 wikisync（topic shard 蒸餾成 wiki 頁）、factlint（facts 去重/去瑣碎/衰減/升格偵測）、wikilint（wiki 過時/孤兒/斷連健康檢查），或要為新 bridge 設計同款 wiki 維運流程時使用
- ms-windows-shell-exit-code-false-positive: 當在 Windows PowerShell 或 cmd.exe 中跑 git clone、pip install、npm install、winget 等命令時，明明工作完成卻收到 exit code 1、stderr 有進度條或 notice、shell tool 回報失敗時使用
- ms-windows-shell-interactive-prompt: 當在 Windows PowerShell 或 cmd.exe 中執行讀檔、列目錄、搜尋指令（Get-Content、type、cat、more）導致 tool call 卡住不回、或在 Kiro agent 與 ACP bridge 裡看到 PowerShell 的互動式參數詢問（例如 Path 參數提示）時使用
- non-engineer-agent-design: Use when designing Custom GPT / Claude Project / Gemini Gem instructions for non-engineers, or when helping someone structure their AI workflow using the 5-piece framework (Agent, Skill, Steering, Hook, KnowHow)
- self-eval-prompt-pattern: Use when designing prompts or instructions that require AI to self-assess quality before delivering output, when setting up automated quality gates in Custom GPT / Project / Gem instructions, or when AI outputs need measurable pass/fail criteria
- skill-creator: Create or update AgentSkills. Use when designing, structuring, or packaging skills with scripts, references, and assets.
- sync-fork-from-upstream: 當使用者要把自己的 fork / 下游倉庫同步到原專案（upstream）的最新更新時使用。觸發語包含「同步原專案」「拿上游更新」「sync upstream」「更新原專案」「原本的倉庫有更新怎麼拉」。涵蓋一次性 upstream remote 設定、半自動同步流程（fetch → 拋棄式分支試合併 → 型別檢查 → 停在衝突/失敗、絕不自動 push）、以及 fork 與 upstream 無共同歷史時的「重新接血脈」做法。
- ui-ux-pro-max: ui-ux-pro-max
- uk-slot-codegen: UK slot codegen 統一入口。使用者提供規格路徑+目標專案路徑，自動串接 adapter→codegen→validation 全流程。支援新專案/增量更新/僅驗證三種模式，每步 checkpoint 可中斷恢復。
- uk-slot-extrabet: UK 老虎機 ExtraBet（額外押注/加倍投注）子系統指南。建立、修改、除錯 ExtraBet 功能，或處理 unshow/replay 還原、單倍率單步驟 / 多倍率兩步驟跳窗變體、多倍率 type 時觸發。涵蓋 framework ExtraBetComponent / extraBetController、專案 ExtraBetWindow 跳窗、ExtraBetEffectManager 動畫、SlotReels 假轉輪整合、GameView 整合多方關係。
- uk-slot-fake-reel-manager: FakeReelManager 假轉輪帶建立、修改、檢查或擴充
- uk-slot-multilang-sync: 把規格 xlsx 的多國語言文字批次同步進 uk 線上老虎機框架各語系的 gameStrings.xml。當使用者要在老虎機專案新增/更新多國語言字串、提到 「gameStrings.xml」「多國語言」「多語系文字」「i18n 字串」「把 xlsx 的翻譯填進去」、 或要為某個 Key 一次寫入多國(常見 26 國)文字時使用。僅適用線上部門 uk 框架 (plist 格式 gameStrings.xml、每語系一目錄、欄標題括號內為語系代碼); 商用部門或其他文字格式不適用。

1	# Preamble Frozen-Snapshot Policy
2	
3	**Audience**: anyone editing `src/sessionManager.ts`, `src/memory.ts`,
4	or anything in the path that feeds Kiro / Codex / Claude's first prompt.
5	**Status**: enforced invariant, not an aspiration.
6	**Inspired by**: Hermes Agent (NousResearch) — same rationale, different
7	plumbing.
8	
9	## The invariant
10	
11	The bridge preamble (tool notes + env block + memory block) is built
12	**once per ACP session** at session creation time. It is **never** rebuilt
13	while a session is live.
14	
15	Concretely:
16	
17	- `sessionManager.create()` calls `buildPreambleWithBreakdown(facts, userId)`
18	  exactly once and freezes the result into `session.memoryPreamble` +
19	  `session.preambleBreakdown`.
20	- `sessionManager.get()` returns the existing `ChatSession` object
21	  untouched when the session is still open (`!client.isClosed`). No
22	  re-read of the facts file, no re-measurement.
23	- `session.preambleInjected` is a latch: preamble text is included in
24	  the **first** `client.prompt()` call for that session and never again.
25	  Subsequent user turns send only the user text.
26	
27	The preamble is only rebuilt when a **new session** is created, which
28	happens in these four paths and no others:
29	
30	1. `get()` sees `existing.client.isClosed` → falls through to `create()`.
31	2. Session is idle past `config.sessionIdleMs` → `sweepIdle()` drops it
32	   and the next `get()` rebuilds.
33	3. Agent process crashes / exits unexpectedly → `exit` handler deletes
34	   the session from the map; next `get()` rebuilds.
35	4. User issues `/restart` or `/drop`, or the bridge shuts down and
36	   restarts.
37	
38	## Why
39	
40	The Kiro / Codex / Claude ACP agents cache their system-prompt prefix
41	aggressively at the LLM provider layer (Anthropic / OpenAI prompt
42	caching, 5-minute TTL on most tiers). If the bridge were to rebuild
43	preamble mid-session and re-send it, **every user turn would invalidate
44	the prefix cache**. For a 3–5 KB preamble that is re-hit 50 times in a
45	long session, that's the difference between `~0 input-token cost for
46	cached content` and `full input-token cost × 50`. It can turn a cheap
47	chat into an expensive one by 1–2 orders of magnitude on the cacheable
48	portion.
49	
50	This is the same reason Hermes Agent's docs hammer the point (see their
51	`prompt-assembly.md` and the `# Prompt Caching Must Not Break` policy
52	in their AGENTS.md): **the only time we alter the cached prefix is when
53	we're already throwing the cache away** (i.e. starting a new session).
54	
55	## Forbidden mid-session operations
56	
57	Do **not** add code that:
58	
59	- Calls `buildPreamble*()` after `create()` has returned.
60	- Mutates `session.memoryPreamble` / `session.preambleBreakdown` in place.
61	- Re-reads `facts-<userId>.md` on every turn to "keep memory fresh".
62	- Reloads `topics.json` / `notebooklm-routing.json` and re-injects them
63	  into the live preamble.
64	- Hot-reloads SKILL.md contents into the system prompt.
65	- Resets `session.preambleInjected = false` to "re-inject" newer context.
66	
67	If you think you need any of the above, you almost certainly want a new
68	session instead — drop the old one (`session.client.close()` and
69	`this.sessions.delete(chatId)`) so the next `get()` rebuilds. That's
70	the right way to invalidate cache.
71	
72	## Allowed mid-session operations
73	
74	These are fine because they don't touch the cached prefix:
75	
76	- Writing to disk: `remember()` / `forget()` / scheduler state / queued
77	  messages / history → all legitimate, all mid-session-safe.
78	- Updating ephemeral state on the `ChatSession` object that is not part
79	  of the preamble: `buffer`, `toolLog`, `charsIn/Out`, `usage`,
80	  `stderrTail`, `inflight`, `lastUsed`.
81	- Appending content to the **current user turn** via tool outputs or
82	  MCP responses. Those are post-prefix, not cached.
83	- Scheduler-triggered commands (`/memorytoskill`, `/topicreview`, etc.)
84	  — these open their own ephemeral ACP client and never touch the
85	  live user session.
86	
87	## Practical consequence for memory writes
88	
89	When the agent (or the bridge, on the user's behalf) calls `remember()`
90	mid-session:
91	
92	1. The fact is appended to `facts-<userId>.md` **immediately**.
93	2. The topic shard at `facts_Topic/<userId>/<topic>.md` is updated
94	   **immediately**.
95	3. The live preamble is **not** updated.
96	4. The agent will see the new fact next time the session is rebuilt —
97	   i.e. after `/restart`, or after `sessionIdleMs` elapses, or after a
98	   manual `/drop`.
99	
100	This is **by design**, not a bug. Document any user-facing "I'll
101	remember that for next time" phrasing accordingly.
102	
103	## If you really need mid-session awareness
104	
105	The correct escape hatches, in order of preference:
106	
107	1. **Tool response**: let the agent call an MCP tool (e.g. `list_facts`)
108	   to query the live state. Tool responses are not cached and always
109	   see disk.
110	2. **Next-turn user message**: queue a synthetic next-turn message like
111	   "Note: just added to memory: …" — this appends after the cached
112	   prefix and is fine.
113	3. **End the session**: if the change is significant enough to need to
114	   be in the prefix, `/restart` is cheap and correct.
115	
116	What you **must not** do is rebuild the preamble silently. That breaks
117	the invariant this document exists to protect.
118	
119	## Change-this-policy checklist
120	
121	Before any PR that touches `buildPreamble*`, `memoryPreamble`,
122	`preambleInjected`, or the `SessionManager.create()` codepath:
123	
124	- [ ] Confirm the change does not cause preamble to be re-emitted on any
125	  turn other than the first of a new session.
126	- [ ] Grep for any new caller of `buildPreamble*()` outside
127	  `sessionManager.ts` — if there is one, it's suspect.
128	- [ ] If the change rebuilds sessions more often (e.g. lowers
129	  `sessionIdleMs`, adds a reason for reset), explicitly justify it
130	  against the prompt-cache cost.
131	- [ ] If you remove or weaken `preambleInjected` latch semantics,
132	  replace this document with an up-to-date one (or delete it and amend
133	  `AGENTS.md`).
134	
135	## Related
136	
137	- `src/sessionManager.ts` — `create()` is the canonical build point.
138	- `src/memory.ts` — `buildPreambleWithBreakdown()` is the only preamble
139	  builder.
140	- `.kiro/steering/windows-shell-safety.md` — sibling steering doc.
141	- Hermes Agent docs, `developer-guide/prompt-assembly.md` — external
142	  reference for the same pattern in a different codebase.

1	# AB Token Protocol — Design Note
2	
3	**Status**: finalized 2026-05-02, ready for AB-2+ implementation.
4	**Scope**: adds three new bridge tokens (`SKILL_USED`, `GOAL_DONE`,
5	`RESTART`) and refactors token extraction into a single
6	`observerTransformer.ts`. RESTART added 2026-05-02 (C3) for agent-
7	driven mid-goal restarts; see §1.3.
8	**Depends on**: `.kiro/steering/preamble-frozen-snapshot.md` (C policy).
9	**Inspired by**: Hermes Agent's `/goal` + Curator `.usage.json`, adapted
10	to our bridge-not-agent architecture.
11	
12	## 1. New tokens
13	
14	### 1.1 `<<GOAL_DONE:reason>>`
15	
16	**Emitted by**: agent, in its streamed reply, when it believes the
17	current `/goal` objective is complete.
18	
19	**Syntax**:
20	```
21	<<GOAL_DONE:完成理由>>
22	```
23	
24	- Single line, MUST NOT span newlines.
25	- `reason`: plain text, ≤ 500 chars (over-length reasons are
26	  **silently truncated** to 500 chars, NOT rejected — see
27	  `BALANCED_REASON_MAX` in `observerTransformer.ts`). MUST NOT
28	  contain `|` or CR/LF. May
29	  contain `<` / `>` only when they form balanced `<<…>>` pairs — the
30	  scanner tracks depth so an agent can safely describe other tokens
31	  inside the reason (e.g.
32	  `<<GOAL_DONE:verified <<RESTART:foo>> end-to-end>>` captures the
33	  whole span atomically and the inner `<<RESTART:foo>>` does NOT fire
34	  as a real restart). Used verbatim in the `✓ Goal achieved` bridge
35	  message.
36	- At most **1 occurrence per reply** — bridge honors the first and
37	  treats the rest as garbage.
38	
39	**Semantics**: presence = "I believe the goal is done". Absence =
40	"keep going". There is no false variant — the simple model is
41	[Hermes's philosophy](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/goals#the-judge) applied to a
42	bridge architecture: fail-open, budget-bounded, simple to get right.
43	
44	**Regex (legacy format reference — actual extractor is a balanced
45	`<<`/`>>` scanner that handles nested tokens inside reason bodies.
46	Note: the handler truncates >500-char reasons, so agents don't need
47	to self-check length — the old 200-char hard reject caused a live
48	bug where agents wrote long "why we're done" summaries and the
49	token stayed verbatim, leaving the goal active in an infinite
50	continuation loop. Fixed 2026-05-03)**:
51	```ts
52	const GOAL_DONE_RE = /<<GOAL_DONE:\s*([^<>|\r\n]{1,500}?)\s*>>/g;
53	```
54	
55	**Bridge behavior**:
56	- Token appears, goal **active** → strip token, stop continuation loop,
57	  send `✓ Goal achieved: <reason>` line, clear goal state.
58	- Token appears, goal **not active** → strip token, log warn, reply
59	  unchanged otherwise.
60	- No token, goal active, turn budget remaining → auto-emit
61	  continuation user-turn (see §3).
62	- No token, goal active, turn budget exhausted → pause loop, send
63	  `⏸ Goal paused — N/N turns used` message.
64	
65	**Position hint**: agent should place the token in the **last few
66	lines** of its reply. The bridge does not enforce position, but
67	reasoning in the tail reduces accidental self-cancellation from
68	in-text examples.
69	
70	### 1.2 `<<SKILL_USED:name|reason>>`
71	
72	**Emitted by**: agent, whenever it **actually consulted** a skill in
73	`${SKILL_DIR}` to produce its reply.
74	
75	**Syntax**:
76	```
77	<<SKILL_USED:skill-name|一句話理由>>
78	```
79	
80	- Single line.
81	- `name`: skill directory name. Charset `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]`, ≤ 60 chars.
82	- `reason`: plain text, ≤ 500 chars (over-length reasons **silently
83	  truncated** to 500, not rejected — policy consistency with
84	  GOAL_DONE/RESTART). MUST NOT contain `|` or CRLF. May
85	  contain `<` / `>` when they form balanced `<<…>>` pairs — the
86	  scanner consumes the outer span atomically so a nested
87	  `<<SEND_FILE:...>>` (or any other token literal) inside the reason
88	  stays in the reason and is NOT extracted as a real side-effect.
89	- Up to **5 tokens per reply** (chosen value, see `AB-1` ASK).
90	  Tokens #6+ in a single reply are left in text as-is so the agent
91	  notices the limit.
92	- Duplicates (same `name` twice in one reply) are de-duped — counted
93	  as one `use_count++`, first `reason` wins.
94	
95	**Regex**:
96	```ts
97	const SKILL_USED_RE =
98	  /<<SKILL_USED:\s*([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,60})\s*\|\s*([^<>|\r\n]{0,500})\s*>>/g;
99	```
100	
101	Mismatch (name with `.`, pipe in reason, etc.) → token left in text,
102	not stripped, not counted. Agent sees its own garbage. Over-length
103	reasons are truncated to 500 chars rather than rejected.
104	
105	**Bridge behavior**:
106	- Strip all well-formed tokens.
107	- For each surviving token:
108	  - If `name` exists in `${SKILL_DIR}/<name>/SKILL.md` → update
109	    `.usage.json[<name>]`: `use_count++`, `last_agent_used_at = now`,
110	    `last_reason = reason`.
111	  - If not → create `.usage.json[<name>] = { orphan: true, ... }`
112	    (flagged but not rejected — agent might be citing a renamed/
113	    deleted skill and the user should see that).
114	
115	**Semantics**: "I read or followed this SKILL.md to help with this
116	reply". Background-knowledge alignment ("I know Kiro well") is **not**
117	a SKILL_USED event. The preamble note (§4) instructs accordingly.
